# UC-22.14.41 · Error Handling — Application Stack Traces Exposing Internals

## Description

Finds logs containing stack traces or SQL fragments that may violate SI-11 error handling by revealing sensitive implementation details.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Route samples to restricted index; (2) work with dev teams to scrub patterns; (3) alert on increases post-release; (4) validate no secrets in messages via regex vault patterns. Indexes required: `index=app`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Java logging.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=app` log4j/json sourcetypes.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Route samples to restricted index; (2) work with dev teams to scrub patterns; (3) alert on increases post-release; (4) validate no secrets in messages via regex vault patterns.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=app earliest=-24h log_level IN ("ERROR","FATAL")
| regex _raw="(?i)(stack trace|SQLException|traceback|ORA-\d{5})"
| stats count by sourcetype, service, host
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Error Handling — Application Stack Traces Exposing Internals (SI-11)** — Finds logs containing stack traces or SQL fragments that may violate SI-11 error handling by revealing sensitive implementation details.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=app` log4j/json sourcetypes. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Java logging. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: app.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=app, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters rows matching a pattern with `regex`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sourcetype, service, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT historian (PI, Proficy) or the vendor HMI/operations console the operator trusts. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (noisy services), Time chart (error spikes), Single value (matches)

## SPL

```spl
index=app earliest=-24h log_level IN ("ERROR","FATAL")
| regex _raw="(?i)(stack trace|SQLException|traceback|ORA-\d{5})"
| stats count by sourcetype, service, host
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (noisy services), Time chart (error spikes), Single value (matches)
